message:
perf(runtime): replace BTreeMap/Vec with HashMap/HashSet on hot paths and fix backoff probing

Multiple runtime subsystems were using BTreeMap for keyed lookups where
ordering was never needed, paying O(log n) per lookup on every poll turn.
This commit migrates them to HashMap for O(1) amortized performance:

Data structure migrations:
- io_driver.rs: `interests: BTreeMap<Token, Interest>` → HashMap
- local.rs: thread-local `LOCAL_TASKS: BTreeMap<TaskId, _>` → HashMap
- obligation_table.rs: `by_holder: BTreeMap<TaskId, _>` → HashMap
- task_table.rs: `stored_futures: BTreeMap<TaskId, StoredTask>` → HashMap

Waker system overhaul (waker.rs):
- `woken: Mutex<Vec<TaskId>>` → `Mutex<HashSet<TaskId>>`
  The old Vec-based wake tracking used linear scan (`contains`) on every
  wake call, making multi-waker scenarios O(n²). HashSet gives O(1) dedup.
- `wake()` now uses `insert()` return value instead of separate contains+push
- `drain_woken()` uses `drain().collect()` instead of `mem::take` to
  match the HashSet API

IoDriver waker optimization (io_driver.rs):
- `update_waker()` now checks `slot.will_wake(&waker)` before cloning
  the waker, avoiding an atomic reference count bump on the common path
  where the same future is re-polled with the same waker

Timer pre-allocation (timer.rs):
- `pop_expired()` pre-allocates the expired vec with capacity 4,
  avoiding reallocation in the common case of small timer batches

Worker backoff fix (scheduler/worker.rs):
- New `pop_backoff_work()` method probes local then global queue directly
  rather than relying on `is_empty()` snapshots that can become stale
  under concurrent producers, fixing a race where the worker would park
  despite immediately-available global work
- Added `test_backoff_probe_pops_global_work` test

GlobalQueue documentation (scheduler/global_queue.rs):
- `len()` and `is_empty()` docs now explicitly note they return
  best-effort snapshots that may be stale under concurrency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -34,13 +34,19 @@ impl GlobalQueue {
         self.inner.pop()
     }
 
-    /// Returns the number of tasks in the queue.
+    /// Returns a best-effort task count snapshot.
+    ///
+    /// Under concurrent producers/consumers this value may change immediately
+    /// after it is observed.
     #[inline]
     pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
         self.inner.len()
     }
 
-    /// Returns true if the queue is empty.
+    /// Returns a best-effort emptiness snapshot.
+    ///
+    /// Under concurrent producers/consumers this hint may become stale
+    /// immediately after it is observed.
     #[inline]
     pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
         self.inner.is_empty()
